product_title: AeroPress Flow Control Filter Cap, No Drip Filter Cap for AeroPress Portable Coffee Press, Specialty Coffee Maker
text:
I typically use the Aeropress Go with a metal filter. The Aeropress Go is fairly small, so usually I do two batches for a full mug of coffee (add coffee grounds, add water, press, pull out plunger, add more water, press again).

Firstly, this does NOT fit the Aeropress Go if you use a metal filter. I ended up with coffee grounds everywhere, not realizing the Flow Control Cap did not actually fit and was not secure. So I had to clean up that mess and get a paper filter instead. It DID work with the paper filter (hence 2 stars instead of 1), BUT when I went in for my second batch, that's where everything went even more wrong. I pulled out the plunger and added water as usual. When I went to press the plunger down, it would not budge. I could see the sides of the Aeropress bulging a little bit each time I tried to press down, as though there was nowhere for the air to go. From there, I had to find a creative way to get my coffee out and my kitchen became a disaster. I felt like one of those people in the infomercials where everything goes wrong. Not the best way to start your morning at 4am. Instant return.

text: This washer uses load sensing technology to determine how much water required for each load. The agitator turns slowly then jets in the amount of water required for the load. Then washing commences. Unfortunately there is so much lateral play in the agitator that it whipsaws side-to-side so far that the whole washing machine lunges back and forth while making loud rumbling sounds. Ours is on a tile floor in a two story condo, so everyone knows when we’re doing laundry. Our washing machine and dryer are behind a set of bi-fold doors. If we don’t jump up and run to the rumbling washer, it moves far enough that we can’t open the bi-fold door. We have no choice but to throw this barely used washer out and buy something else.

product_title: Whirlpool W10830162 OEM French Refrigerator Door Gasket Part-Replaces 12723206AP, W10179332, W10179332N, and More
text: I purchased the whirlpool replacement gasket (I have a Maytag refrigerator) at half the price through Amazon that I would pay from Whirlpool. As others have said, I let one gasket relax for a day unfolded, then smoothed it out using my hair dryer and fingers for about 5 minutes. My gasket is magnetic, so removing the old one took less than a minute. Installing the new one was about 5 minutes as I ran my fingers over the groove and made sure all the edges and corners were right. I used the dryer again for another minute to let it relax.  The second gasket came right out of the box, took about 5 minutes to relax with the dryer and snapped it in quicker than the first one.  The seal was not as firm at first, but the next morning it was normal.  I’m glad I looked it up by model number on the manufacturer website first, so I new for sure what the part number was, but even if it was a screw on kind, I would try doing it myself, instead of calling for service.

text:
Wool is the best natural fiber for activewear. Let's give these wool socks by Merino.tech a try.

What's good:
Crew/mid-calf wool socks for a wide variety of uses. I'd use them for hiking, snowboarding, skiing, sledding, etc.
Decent quality merino wool and fabrication.
Wool fiber is superior to polyester/polypropylene/synthetic fibers in terms of warmth, breathability, less odor retention, and repelling water.
Fits true to size: L (9-12) fits my size 8 feet almost perfectly, with slight room to spare in the heels. That's alright because even processed merino wool will shrink slightly after washing.
Easy care: hand/machine wash, line dry.

Drawbacks:
Wool socks cost more, are less durable than synthetic fibers, takes longer to dry, and are vulnerable to moths if not washed after use.
Socks have medium thickness and are not suitable for temperatures lower than 40°F.
$29.99 (Mar. 21, 2023) for one pair of wool socks is too high. Competitors sell 2 or 3 pairs for approx. that price.

text: I went through three defective Keurig single cup coffeemakers that had a problem in which they stopped early in the brewing cycle.  Keurig’s response was to offer to replace the coffeemaker, and to dispose of the defective unit.  That’s not a very environmentally friendly practice, so I decided to return the coffeemaker to Amazon.  After doing some research on the internet, I ordered the Cuisinart  coffeemaker that uses single cup K-pods, and have been very happy with its design, ease of use and dependability.  The water reservoir holds more water than the Keurig, so I don’t have to refill it as often.  It also has a plastic K-pod container that stores in the unit, so you have the option to grind your own coffee.  It has options to select the size of cup you wish to brew, and has both a hot water button for tea or cocoa or a rinse button to clean the nozzle.  It’s a superior single cup coffeemaker.

product_title: Gevi Household V2.0 Countertop Nugget Ice Maker | Self Cleaning Pellet Ice Machine | Open and Pour Water Refill | Store Ice Up to 24 Hours | Stainless Steel Housing | Fits Under Wall Cabinet | White
text:
I wasn't sold on this ice maker at first. My friend has a lower-end model from a different brand that sells for around $100, so when I saw that this one was going for over $500, I could not fathom what made it that much better to justify the extra cost. I was a total skeptic until I tried it.

If you are someone who only occasionally uses ice and isn't picky about ice, this probably is not your machine. This was ordered primarily for someone who drinks like a gallon or more of iced tea per day.

Both machines are similar in "capacity" and they take about the same length of time to create ice. BUT, the cheaper ice maker produces "wet" ice if that makes sense and is insanely loud. This one operates much quieter and the ice is finished, plus it keeps it colder for longer. The ice basket is also 3-4 times bigger.

The quality of ice is different too. The description says it's like Chick-fil-A ice... I don't know about that because I don't go there, but it reminds me of Sonic ice - like what you'd get with a slush. That means the ice nuggets fit together more compactly and you wind up with a colder beverage faster and the temperature of the drink is more even... Again, I'm using mine mostly for fresh-brewed iced tea, so you basically add a ton of cubes to chill it down at the start and then some in each glass... and your first glasses aren't going to be cold. With traditional ice, you get warm patches in your drink. With this kind of ice, it's an even chill.

Also a word of warning, yes, it's really heavy. I didn't have any trouble getting it in the door or on the counter but a smaller person or someone with limited lifting ability will need help.

But the bottom line is that, yes, if you use a lot of ice, or you're a snob about the quality of your ice/ how the ice impacts your drinks, this is worth the extra money. It also looks nice and doesn't take up a whole lot of space.

text:
The range hood that this replaced was exactly 30" wide and fit nice and snug into the mounting area. This unit is only 29" wide and leaves a 1/2" gap on both sides of the unit. The unit is also not as tall as the one replaced; you can see in the last picture the white, untiled area just below the hood. The tile was done up to the bottom of the previously installed hood.

Pros: The LED light is brighter than the previous unit with the incandescent light. The fan is speedy, yet not too loud. The unit comes well-protected with the plastic protective coating and once the coating is removed, the stainless steel is very aesthetically pleasing. The price is very competitive and reasonable for this unit.

Cons: The length of 1" short is terribly annoying and really knocks down the rating of this unit. There is only one hole to run the power cord up to the power source; I'd like to see a second hole made available on the left side. Making sure you don't pinch the cord when installing was a pain.

Overall, make sure you check your measurements exactly, since there is no forgiveness for being short an inch. While installation was a bit of a pain, it was pain to install the previous one I had in there, so all things being equal, install is a bit of a pain no matter which brand you buy. Had the width been as advertised, this would rate a 4 or maybe even 5 stars.

product_title: Soundcore Anker Life Q20 Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Headphones, Wireless Over Ear Bluetooth Headphones, 40H Playtime, Hi-Res Audio, Deep Bass, Memory Foam Ear Cups, for Travel, Home Office
text:
These headphones are great!

They feel fairly light, compared to my old, heavy pair, but not as light as my bose wired, non-bluetooth, non noise-canceling pair.

These have a feature that I really like: when I turn noise canceling (NC) on, the volume automatically goes down a bit. When I turn NC back off, the volume goes back up. This makes it so if I enter a loud area (such as a bus) and turn on NC, the headphones will block out a lot of the noise, but my eardrums are safe from spontaneous explosion (A bit of an exaggeration, I know, but I do have sensitive hearing, and having that sudden noise level difference can suck). I do have to adjust the volume a little, but that is not a big deal for me.

I also love that when I turn the headphones off, the voice telling me it's off is quiet enough that I can be wearing the headphones without having my ears blasted. With my old pair, I had to remember to take them off before turning them on/off, or when connecting Bluetooth.

Now for something that I am fine with, but should be noted for some other people: the ear-cup fit. These are over-the-ear headphones, but they are designed so the ear should be tucked behind the foam. I personally found no problems with this once I got used to it (which took about 10 minutes the first time, and it was fine every time after) but I can understand some people not liking it.

I think the sound quality is good, but I am not knowledgeable about this sort if thing, so I will leave it for other people to review.

All in all, this is a great product, and definitely worth my money. I wear these for several hours a day (though with some breaks in between) and find it takes a long time before they feel a little uncomfortable. I have NOT tested these with my regular glasses, but with my old pair of headphones I would get headaches after 20-30 minutes due to the arms of the glasses frame being pressed into my skull. My sunglasses on the other hand are comfortable to wear with both headphone pairs, more so with this new one than the old (my sunglasses have taller, thinner arms, so there is more surface area against my head, therefore not as much pressure in a small area).

I hope you have a wonderful day!

product_title: Movo GB-U70Y Underwater Diving Rig for GoPro Hero with Cold Shoe Mounts, Wrist Strap - Works with HERO3, HERO4, HERO5, HERO6, HERO7, HERO8, and Osmo Action Cam - Scuba GoPro Accessory (Yellow)
text:
I'm a novice at setting up a camera rig which I'm hoping will give me more stability for underwater videos and photos, as well as video lighting, with my Olympus TG camera.
This is the larger size tray and it easily fits the TG6 without or with a housing. It also works with a quick release plate but it has to be a relatively flat one, some are too tall.

There are several issues that I'm still working to rectify.
You must always have a quarter handy because the main screw lacks a D-ring to turn. It also doesn't come out due to a locking washer - which is good for not losing the screw, but makes it impossible to use the other screw holes in the base because it projects above the base.

Also, if you only use the center single screw there's a tendency for the camera to spin, I fear stripping the threads if I tighten too much.

Finally, both sides have an odd little plastic plug that covers 2 screws, but the plug keeps falling out & getting lost even when setting the rig up.

I'll probably keep this because it is the simplest tray I can find, doesn't require assembly or special ball mount parts.

product_title: AOSUODI Smart Watch for Women Men,Fitness Watch 1.44'' Touch Screen with Heart Rate Sleep Monitor,Blood Oxygen,Tracker for Health,Running,Activity Trackers Smartwatch Sports for iOS Android White
text:
I could have given this smart watch a five star for an incentive but that will not be a sincere and honest opinion.

The watch itself is beautiful but it is not a five-star watch. It beeps when I get messages: texts, WhatsApp, etc. It also gives notification for calls. I am able to read my messages. I also like the fact that it can be charged on a computer.

Cons: This smart watch came with no manual and gives inaccurate step counts.. The first day I used it, I went to bed at 10:40pm and got up 6am and it reported about 523 steps. I do not do much walking at work but a at the end of the day, it reported over 5000 steps. The app on my phone recorded about 300. On my second day, I was charging it and forgot it in my computer but in the morning, it had reported 62 steps, 7281 by 1:32pm, and 17577 steps at 9:30. Meanwhile the app on my phone recorded 2100 steps. Maybe this smart watch is so sensitive and is reading my wrist movements for steps or is using another kind of measurement unit. For instance: kilos and pounds. If this is not the case, then the step recordings are way off, because of that, I didn't even bother to check the accuracy of the BPM numbers since waking can have an impact on BPM but it's stayed on 70 for the two days I since getting it.

Nonetheless, with the price, I think it is a decent watch but if step counts is the main reason you want to get a smart watch, then this is not for you, unless it is fixed.
